memory.summary: I'm only 10% away from finishing this assignment with plenty of time, so I'll keep my focus here and grab a quick meal with the group before submitting.
Wait, the deadline is in ten minutes? I thought I had hours left. My group lunch just got way more complicated.
I'm genuinely shocked that the deadline is ten minutes away after I just planned a group meal. My focus was so locked on finishing the last 10% that I completely lost track of time.

I can't finish this in ten minutes without ruining my lunch with the group. I'm going to submit what I have and apologize later, because keeping them happy matters more than a perfect grade right now.
I'm torn between submitting this incomplete work to save our lunch plans and stressing over the grade. My group matters more, but I hate feeling like I'm letting everyone down.

I'm stuck between my hunger for social connection and the guilt of failing this assignment. My impulsive need to preserve the group moment is making me dread the inevitable conflict with the professor.
The guilt of letting the professor down is eating at me, but I can't risk missing our lunch. I'll just submit what I have now and deal with the consequences later.
I'm eating lunch with my friends while the unfinished assignment sits on my phone, and honestly, I don't care enough to fix it right now.
The guilt is loud, but the lunch feels too good to ruin; I'm just going to let this slide again and hope for the best.
Lunch with the crew feels essential right now, so I'm pushing the assignment guilt aside again and hoping I can fix it later without consequences.
The guilt is screaming, but the lunch with my crew feels non-negotiable right now; I'm just going to hope the deadline waits until I get back.
The guilt is screaming, but the lunch with my crew feels non-negotiable right now; I'm just going to hope the deadline waits until I get back.

desires: To stay at the table with my crew until they all leave, because missing this part of the day would feel like a betrayal of our bond.
To convince myself that the assignment is 'done enough' or that I can just submit it later without getting caught, so I don't have to break up the good vibes.
To ignore the rising panic in my chest and tell myself that the deadline isn't actually happening right now.
To get home quickly so I can finally collapse into bed and escape the guilt eating away at me.

goals.description: Stay fully present at the table with cohort_3; finish eating and chat without checking my phone or thinking about the assignment.
goals.rationale: Affiliation is my north star (0.5 weight). Being distracted by work while my friends are right here feels like a betrayal of our closeness. I need to soak up this time now because if I mess this up, it's gone forever, whereas the grade is just a number.

goals.description: Finish the 'urgent_submit' assignment just enough to hit the deadline today so I can stop spiraling about tomorrow's panic.
goals.rationale: My impulsive nature makes me want to procrastinate until the last second, but the guilt loop is getting too loud. If I don't knock this out now, the looming deadline will ruin my lunch anyway. It's a compromise: get the task done fast so I can go back to being a good friend, even if I'm rushing.

goals.description: Hit 'submit' on the assignment right now so I can stop feeling like a slacker and go eat with my friends.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive (relationships) is screaming to be with cohort_3, but my assertiveness means I won't let them wait or feel guilty. The impulsive bias wants immediate relief from the screen, so finishing this 10% chunk is the only way to get back to the social connection I value most.

desires: Finish this assignment and submit it before 13:30 so I can eat lunch with the group without feeling guilty or rushed.
Not look like I'm slacking off to the professor while they're waiting for my work, even though I just want to grab food.
Get this done quickly so I can go back to hanging out with my close friends instead of staring at a screen.
